domain: The Skelet 17 non-halting proof (bbchallenge): Savask's counter-list abstraction (Mar 2023), Chris Xu's Gray-code/embanked-state proof (Jul 2024), and the Coq-BB5 formalization by mxdys (~6,900 lines, BB5_Skelet17.v) — the hardest single machine in the BB(5) determination, described by the BB5 paper as 'obfuscated Gray code' and suspected irregular (no regular CTL invariant exists).

translations.technique: Savask's sweep-section reduction: TM -> unbounded-dimension counter-list rewriting system (levels 0-2 of the Coq proof)
translations.what_it_actually_does: Savask observes that Skelet 17's head, between visits to the right tape edge, performs one full left-right sweep, and that at every right-edge visit the tape lies in the regular language .(10)^{n1} 1 (10)^{n2} ... 1 (10)^{nk} B>. He encodes each such configuration as an integer list (n1,...,nk) and computes the first-return map of the TM to this section as 13 list-rewriting rules (S1-S6, E1-E3, O1-O6), later normalized by Xu/mxdys to 5 rules on (a_l,...,a_1,a_0): Increment (rightmost-odd-entry's left neighbor +1, a_0 -1), Halve (drop a_0=−1 case: (a_l,...,a_1,-1) -> (a_l,...,a_1), i.e. list shift), Zero, Overflow, Halt. The Coq file makes this a certified bisimulation: relations Increment/Halve/Zero/Overflow on nat*(list nat), a toConfig relation, and lemmas Increment_toConfig etc. proving every abstract transition is realized by concrete TM steps (c1 -->* c3). Notably the halting rule survives into the abstraction (Halt iff state (0,0,2a_{l-2},...,2a_0)), so non-halting of the abstract system transfers exactly.
translations.math_translation: Let M be the TM step map on configurations, X = {lower(a) : a a finite integer list} the (regular) section. Theorem: the blank-tape orbit meets X infinitely often unless it halts, and the induced (first-return) map R: X -> X ∪ {HALT} is exactly the 5-rule counter system; formally there is a relation-preserving simulation (a functional bisimulation up to time reparametrization) between (Orb(c0), M) and (N x N*, R). In dynamics language: computing the Poincaré return map of a symbolic flow to a regular cross-section, and discovering it to be a counter automaton with unboundedly many counters (a VASS-like system with zero-tests, parity guards, dimension growth, and one non-conservative rule). Savask also isolates the conserved/monotone quantities: total sum invariant except +1 under Overflow (O1) and +1/+2 under counter-reset (O6); rightmost entry strictly decreasing except at resets.

nearest_known_math.theory: Induced/first-return (Poincaré) maps and Kakutani skyscrapers in ergodic theory
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by — the construction is literally an induced map on a cross-section; the community reinvented it as 'macro machines'/'chain step' acceleration
nearest_known_math.ref: standard; cf. Petersen, Ergodic Theory, ch. on induced transformations

nearest_known_math.theory: Macro machines / tape-run acceleration in busy beaver work
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by — direct descendant of Marxen-Buntrock's run-length acceleration; Savask's contribution is doing it by hand at the right section and reading off closed-form rules

nearest_known_math.theory: Regular model checking (configurations as regular languages, transitions as transducers)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — the section is a regular language and R is a rational relation on run-length encodings; but RMC iterates transducers and cannot express the counter magnitudes' unbounded arithmetic that matters here

nearest_known_math.theory: Vector addition systems with states, counter machines with zero tests
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — the abstract system is a counter machine, but with parity guards, dimension growth and resets; reachability theory (VASS) and WSTS/wqo methods (Finkel-Schnoebelen) do not apply since monotonicity fails

translations.fusion_opportunity: Fuse with automata learning + regular model checking: an algorithm that (i) detects a recurrent regular section from simulation traces, (ii) learns the return map as list-rewriting rules with symbolic (Presburger) effects, (iii) checks the learned rules by bounded bisimulation. This would mechanize the one step of the Skelet 17 pipeline that was pure human pattern-staring, and produce counter-system presentations for other holdout-like machines automatically (sligocki's busy-beaver repo does fragments of this ad hoc).
translations.collatz_application: The accelerated Syracuse map T(n)=(3n+1)/2^{v2(3n+1)} IS this technique applied to Collatz (induced map on the odd-integers section). The transferable content is the second-level move: re-encode iterates by their binary run-length lists and compute the induced rewriting rules of Syracuse on run-length tuples. Stérin-Woods (arXiv 2007.06979) prove the Collatz process in base 2 is a quasi-cellular automaton with local rules, so a Savask-style finite rule table on run-length lists exists; deriving it explicitly would give our certificate program the same raw object (a counter-list rewriting system) that made Skelet 17 attackable — certificates then talk about integer lists with Presburger side conditions instead of raw integers.

translations.technique: Gray-code decoding of the parity vector: an explicit semiconjugacy onto a counter-with-halving automaton (Savask's observation, Xu Def 2.1, Coq level 3)
translations.what_it_actually_does: Savask noticed in traces that 'the behavior of the machine is driven by parity changes of these numbers and they are not random': rule O4 finds the rightmost ODD entry and increments its left neighbor — i.e., the parity word (a_i mod 2) changes in exactly one position per Increment. He recognized this as reflected binary Gray code with the rightmost entry serving simultaneously as countdown variable and parity bit. Xu formalizes: n(S) := GrayCode^{-1}(parities of a_l...a_1), sigma(S) := ±1 by parity of the total sum, l(S) := |S|-1, with the closed form GrayCode digit_i(n) = <n/2^i> mod 2 (nearest integer, half-up) — equivalent to the classical g_i(n) = floor((n+2^i)/2^{i+1}) mod 2. The Coq version (grey_to_binary = suffix-XOR of map odd) proves the exact update table: Increment: n -> n+sigma, sigma fixed; Halve: n -> floor(n/2), sigma -> -sigma, l -> l-1; Zero: n -> 2^l - 1, l -> l+2, sigma -> -1... ; Overflow: n: 2^l-1 -> 0, l -> l+1; Halt only from (n,sigma)=(0,-1). Crucially the observable (n,l,sigma) also CONSTRAINS which rule can fire (Overflow requires n=2^l-1 and sigma=+1), so the opaque tuple dynamics is scheduled by a rigid 3-variable machine. The fiber data (the actual magnitudes a_i) evolve as a cocycle: Xu's Prop 2.2 / Coq Increments_a show a_i changes by d_i(n,n') = |<n/2^i> - <n'/2^i>| = the number of times Gray digit i-1 flips as the counter runs from n to n', computed by divpow2r(n,i) = floor((n+2^i)/2^{i+1}).
translations.math_translation: Theorem (implicit): Let (X,R) be the counter-list system. Define phi: X -> N x N x {±1}, phi(S) = (n(S), l(S), sigma(S)) as above. Then phi is a semiconjugacy from R onto a partial map F on N x N x {±1} given by the table above; moreover the rule applied at S is determined by phi(S) together with a two-bit predicate (a_1- or a_0-degeneracy). The Increment sub-dynamics, compactified (n in Z_2), is the dyadic adding machine n -> n+sigma read in Gray code coordinates: for all m and all i, digit i flips at exactly the times m ≡ 2^i mod 2^{i+1} (ruler/odometer schedule), and the fiber motion is the Birkhoff sum of cylinder-indicator functions along the odometer orbit (a_i(t) = a_i(0) ± #{s<=t : digit i-1 flips at s}). So the proof exhibits the TM's blank-tape orbit as an extension of a zero-entropy odometer-with-shift system: base = (n,l,sigma) counter dynamics (odometer increments interleaved with the shift n -> floor(n/2)), fiber = Z^infinity-valued digit-counting cocycle. To answer the posed question directly: yes, the Gray-code structure is literally an odometer factor of the return dynamics during Increment phases — but the full factor is richer than an odometer: it is an odometer interleaved with halving/reset, i.e., exactly a 'binary counter automaton', and the orbit closure of the rule-trace is plausibly a Toeplitz-like almost-1:1 extension of the dyadic odometer (unstated and unproven by anyone).

nearest_known_math.theory: Reflected binary Gray code and counter theory
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the code itself (single-flip property, ruler-sequence flip schedule, closed-form digit formulas are classical)

nearest_known_math.theory: Odometers, Toeplitz systems, maximal equicontinuous factors
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — supplies exactly the right vocabulary (adding machine, almost-1:1 extension, digit-flip = wheel-turning schedule) but has never been applied to busy beaver machines; my searches for 'odometer factor Turing machine/busy beaver' found nothing
nearest_known_math.ref: Downarowicz, 'Survey of odometers and Toeplitz flows', Contemp. Math. 385 (2005); Markley-Paul

nearest_known_math.theory: Topological dynamics of Turing machines; the SMART machine's minimal substitutive dynamics
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — Cassaigne-Ollinger-Torres-Avilés constructed BY DESIGN a reversible TM whose trace subshift is minimal and substitutive (odometer-adjacent, zero entropy); Skelet 17 is a found-in-the-wild, non-reversible, halting-candidate instance of the same phenomenon, discovered by reverse engineering rather than construction
nearest_known_math.ref: Cassaigne, Ollinger, Torres-Avilés, 'A Small Minimal Aperiodic Reversible Turing Machine', JCSS 2017; Kůrka, 'On topological dynamics of Turing machines', TCS 1997

nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — every side condition in the proof (powers of 2, v_2, divpow2r, ctzS) is definable in (N,+,V_2); the invariant is thus 'automatic' over integer coordinates even though the configuration language over tape words is irregular — this recoding is exactly what defeats the regular-CTL deciders' limitation
nearest_known_math.ref: Bruyère, Hansel, Michaux, Villemaire, 'Logic and p-recognizable sets of integers', 1994

translations.fusion_opportunity: State and prove the compactified theorem the proof implies but never says: the rule-trace of Skelet 17's blank-tape orbit is a Toeplitz sequence whose orbit closure is an almost-1:1 extension of the dyadic odometer Z_2 (eigenvalue group = dyadic rationals). Payoffs: (1) it converts the community's informal 'irregularity' argument (BB5 paper: 'informal irregularity arguments have been given for... Skelet #17') into a theorem — a Toeplitz trace with unboundedly growing counters forces a non-regular reachable-configuration language; (2) it poses a clean classification problem mathematicians can own: which small TMs have zero-entropy (Toeplitz/substitutive/odometer-factor) blank-tape orbit closures? The BB5 holdout taxonomy (counters, shift-overflow counters, Finned machines) suggests the answer is 'essentially all machines that survive regular deciders without being Collatz-like', which would be a genuine structural theorem about the halting problem's hard instances.
translations.collatz_application: Two-sided. Negative side (calibration): the 2-adic Collatz extension is topologically conjugate to the full shift on Z_2 (Lagarias 1985; explicit conjugacy Bernstein-Lagarias 1996) and Bernoulli for Haar measure — positive entropy, hence NO nontrivial odometer/equicontinuous factor globally. Factor-detection therefore cannot apply to Syracuse as a whole; Skelet 17 was solvable precisely because its one relevant orbit is zero-entropy. Positive side: individual orbits and engineered sections can still have odometer factors. Concrete program for our certificates: along accelerated Syracuse orbits, log the 'which run-length coordinate changed' index sequence (the analogue of the Gray flip schedule) and test it for 2-automaticity/ruler structure via automata learning or Walnut; any hit yields a candidate recoding under which the orbit's parity data becomes a counter, and then non-divergence/cycle statements about that orbit family become sentences of Büchi arithmetic — decidable, hence certifiable. This is the precise sense in which 'find odometer factors of the accelerated Syracuse dynamics' is a well-posed method: it can only certify structured exceptional orbits, which is exactly what a divergence certificate for a Collatz-like map must be (cf. the open divergence of the 5x+1 orbit of 7 — believed random, so believed outside this method's reach; the method's true habitat is Collatz-like maps arising from TM holdouts, per Michel's dictionary).
sources_read: web searches: SMART machine (sciencedirect S0022000016301003), 'odometer factor busy beaver' (no hits — calibration)

translations.technique: Xu's embanked invariant + accelerated return map N' whose itinerary is a 2-adic valuation (ruler) sequence (Xu §3-4, Coq levels 4-5)
translations.what_it_actually_does: Xu takes a second cross-section inside the abstract system: 'empty' states E (n=0, sigma=-1, next rule Zero) and the first-return map N to this section. Naive analysis fails, so he defines an invariant discovered from the failure: E is 'embanked' if its return word T_E is exactly [Zero, Increments, Halve, Increments, Halve, Increments] (one Zero, two Halves, rest Increments), and 'weakly embanked' with extra Halves allowed. Prop 3.4 characterizes weak embankedness by two inequalities: a_0(E) < 2^{2k+1}-1 and a_1(E) < 3·2^{2k}. Prop 3.6 proves the upgrade a'_j(N(E[1])) - a_j(E[1]) = 2 d_j(h_2+1, h_2) >= 0 — a digit-distance monotonicity inequality — so weakly embanked return states of embanked states are again embanked. On embanked states N acts as N(E) = E[i]: a single coordinate a_i += 2, and the next index is given by 2-adic valuations of two auxiliary counters h_1, h_2 (the values of n after the first/second Halve): i' = v_2(h_1) if 0-rooted, v_2(h_2+1)+1 if 1-rooted (Cor 3.8; Coq: ctzS(h_1-1) vs S(ctzS h_2), where ctzS(n)=v_2(n+1)). N' accelerates ceil((i+1)/2) applications of N into one 'embanked_batch'. Over an epoch, h_1 decrements from 2^{2k}-1 while h_2 increments from 2^{2k}, so the index itinerary consists of TWO INTERLEAVED RULER SEQUENCES ('fractal structure 1,121,1213121,121312141213121,... generated by ctzS' — Coq-BB5 md), switching lines on parity changes, kept synchronized by the identities ctzS(2^i+m)=ctzS(m) and ctzS(2^i-m-2)=ctzS(m) (Coq lemmas ctzS_add, ctzS_sub, BB5_Skelet17.v:4218-4274). Prop 4.3 then counts total increments per coordinate over a whole epoch: kappa_{E->E'}(j) = #{e in (m,m'] : 2^{j-1} | e} — Legendre-style counting of multiples of powers of 2 in an interval.
translations.math_translation: Theorem (implicit): on the section Sigma_0 = {empty states}, restricted to the forward-invariant embanked family V (defined by the Prop 3.4 inequalities plus rootedness), the return map N is conjugate to a skew product over a rank-one, zero-entropy base: base points are counter pairs (h_1,h_2) with parity-driven switching, base dynamics h_1 -> h_1-1 / h_2 -> h_2+1, and the fiber map adds 2·e_{i(h)} where i(h) is read from the ruler sequence r(m)=v_2(m+1). Equivalently: the return dynamics is an explicit Vershik (adic) map on a two-vertex-per-level Bratteli diagram (the 0-rooted/1-rooted split IS the two-vertex structure), and the coordinate increments are the ergodic sums of cylinder indicators — so all epoch-level accounting reduces to Legendre/Delange digit-counting identities. The invariance argument is a k-induction: V is NOT closed under single abstract steps, only under the composite accelerated map N' — with the certificate of closure being a discrete monotonicity inequality for the digit-distance function d_j. Quantifier form: for all k, for all m in [0, 2^{2k}-2] with v_2(m') odd..., there exists a unique 1-rooted embanked E' with h(N^{-1}(E')) = (2^{2k}-m'-1, 2^{2k}+m') and a_i(E') = a_i(E)+2d for i in {0,1} (Xu Prop 4.1).

nearest_known_math.theory: Vershik adic transformations / Bratteli-Vershik models of Cantor minimal systems
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — the accelerated return map with ruler itinerary is a hand-rolled adic map; the tower-of-sections structure is Kakutani-Rokhlin; but the theory is developed for compact minimal systems, not for one non-compact orbit with growing counters
nearest_known_math.ref: Herman, Putnam, Skau, 'Ordered Bratteli diagrams...', Int. J. Math 1992

nearest_known_math.theory: Digit-counting sums and carry propagation (Legendre's formula, Delange's method)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by — kappa(j) = #multiples of 2^{j-1} in an interval and divpow2r are classical; the proof re-derives them from scratch (Coq divpow2r_* lemmas)
nearest_known_math.ref: Legendre 1808; Delange, 'Sur la fonction sommatoire de la fonction somme des chiffres', 1975

nearest_known_math.theory: k-induction and acceleration in infinite-state model checking (flat counter systems, Presburger acceleration)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — 'invariant closed only under the accelerated composite map, verified by an auxiliary inequality' is exactly k-induction + loop acceleration; but those tools handle Presburger effects, while the effects here need v_2 and powers of 2 (Büchi arithmetic), outside standard accelerators like FAST
nearest_known_math.ref: Sheeran, Singh, Stålmarck, FMCAD 2000; Boigelot & Wolper; Finkel & Leroux, 'How to compose Presburger-accelerations', FSTTCS 2002

nearest_known_math.relationship: disjoint-from — no wqo monotonicity anywhere: rules have zero-tests, parity guards, and the order-violating Halve/Zero; the proof never uses coverability-style arguments, confirming WSTS does not subsume this technique
nearest_known_math.ref: Finkel & Schnoebelen, 'Well-structured transition systems everywhere!', TCS 2001

translations.fusion_opportunity: Recast levels 4-5 as a Bratteli-Vershik construction: two vertices per level (0-rooted/1-rooted), edges ordered by the ruler schedule, fiber cocycle = Add2. Standard return-word/tower machinery (Durand-Host-Skau) would then replace Xu's six nested inductions with reusable lemmas, and — the real prize — make the construction parametric: a library-level theorem 'any counter-list system whose return itinerary is generated by finitely many ctzS-driven lines with dyadic synchronization admits a closed-form epoch map' would mechanize the hardest 2,000 lines of the Coq proof and apply to future holdouts (BB(6) machines like Antihydra will need exactly this altitude of tooling).
translations.collatz_application: Directly imports as a certificate FORMAT: allow invariants that are only k-inductive relative to an accelerated composite map, with closure certified by digit-distance inequalities, and with all arithmetic side conditions restricted to Büchi arithmetic (N,+,V_2) so a decision procedure can discharge them. For accelerated Syracuse: the analogue of (h_1,h_2) bookkeeping is tracking v_2(3n+1) along the orbit; a certificate that a family of residue-class sections has ruler-structured v_2-itineraries would give closed-form epoch maps for Collatz-like systems. Realistic first target: the Collatz-like functions Michel extracted from BB champions (michel2015), where orbits from specific seeds are exactly the structured, zero-entropy kind this machinery certifies.

translations.technique: Self-similar epoch renormalization: Base_k ->* Base_{k+1} with exactly one Overflow per epoch (Xu Thm 1.3 endgame, Coq levels 6-7)
translations.what_it_actually_does: The proof closes by exhibiting an explicit self-similar family S_k = (0,2,4,...,2^{2k},0) (Coq: Base k ~ lower [0; 2^{2k}; ...; 4; 2; 0], reached from blank tape in 205 steps for k=1) and proving S_k |-> S_{k+1} using exactly one Overflow rule per epoch. The epoch consists of: one embanked_batch from Base k initializing (h_1,h_2)=(2^{2k}-1, 2^{2k}); ~2^{2k} accelerated N'steps driving (h_1,h_2) to (1, 2^{2k+1}-2) with per-coordinate totals given by the Legendre counts (a_j(E'') = 3·2^{2k-j+1}-2+..., computed exactly); then the unique non-embanked event ZIHIO (Zero-Increments-Halve-Increments-Overflow) — the single Overflow — which expands the list length from 2k+1 to 2k+3; then two more embanked_batches landing exactly on Base (k+1). Halting is excluded because Halt requires (n,sigma)=(0,-1) at a state whose entire tail is even, and the invariant plus the countdown accounting shows the countdown variable always exhausts first (Savask had already identified this as THE crux: 'it seems crucial to prove that the countdown variable should run out before the machine counts down to the all-zero number'). Non-halting follows since Base configs at different k are distinct (Coq Base_ne) and Sk_closed composes forever.
translations.math_translation: Theorem: for all k>=1, R^{t_k}(S_k) = S_{k+1} for some finite t_k, with the orbit segment avoiding Halt; hence the blank-tape orbit is infinite and visits (0,2,4,...,2^{2k},0) for every k (Xu Thm 1.3). Structurally: the blank-tape orbit is SELF-INDUCED — the first-return map of the dynamics to the family {Base k} is conjugate (after the coordinate dilation a_i -> 2a_i, l -> l+2) to the original system one scale up. This is a renormalization fixed-point statement: the orbit is infinitely renormalizable with combinatorics of period doubling (epoch lengths ~ 4^k, one Overflow per epoch playing the role of the once-per-scale carry). The closest classical picture: the Feigenbaum attractor, where the infinitely renormalizable unimodal map restricted to its Cantor attractor is topologically conjugate to the dyadic adding machine — here the TM sits at the analogous 'edge of halting', with the odometer appearing as the rigid factor and the renormalization providing the induction that replaces a (nonexistent) regular invariant.

nearest_known_math.theory: Self-induced minimal Cantor systems and substitutive systems (return words, S-adic representations)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — 'self-induced implies substitutive' (Durand's characterization) is exactly the phenomenon; but that theory is for compact minimal systems, and no published work applies it to halting problems of specific machines
nearest_known_math.ref: Durand, 'Linearly recurrent subshifts have a finite number of non-periodic subshift factors', ETDS 2000; Durand-Host-Skau 1999

nearest_known_math.theory: Adding machines inside renormalizable one-dimensional dynamics (Feigenbaum attractor conjugate to the dyadic odometer)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — same architecture (odometer factor + period-doubling renormalization) in a completely different category; nobody has drawn this analogy for busy beaver holdouts as far as searching reveals
nearest_known_math.ref: de Melo & van Strien, One-Dimensional Dynamics; Bruin-Keller-Nowicki-van Strien, Ann. Math. 1996

nearest_known_math.theory: Michel's Collatz-like classification of busy beaver champions
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps by contrast — Michel's dictionary (champions simulate Collatz-like exponential arithmetic maps, halting = open number theory) does NOT cover Skelet 17: its abstraction is a zero-entropy counter tower, not an arithmetic map with congruence branching; Skelet 17 shows the holdout ecology contains a second, rigid phylum
nearest_known_math.ref: Michel, 'Problems in number theory from busy beaver competition', LMCS 11(4:10) 2015 (local PDF, pages 1-3 read)

nearest_known_math.theory: Aperiodicity proofs for constructed TMs via self-simulation (SMART)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — SMART's aperiodicity/minimality proof also proceeds by scale self-similarity of the machine's behavior; Skelet 17's is the same argument found in nature and formalized

translations.fusion_opportunity: The fusion is a conjecture worth stating publicly: every 5-state machine that survives all regular (CTL/DFA) deciders without being Collatz-like has an eventually substitutive rule-trace — i.e., the sieve of regular deciders selects for infinitely renormalizable, zero-entropy orbits, and odometer factors are the generic mechanism of provable non-halting. If true, non-halting certificates have a universal format: (substitution/renormalization operator, seed family, closure proof), generalizing counters, shift-overflow counters, and Skelet 17 simultaneously. This is the theorem-shaped version of the empirical observation that 'TM holdouts keep containing odometer factors', and it is exactly the kind of statement (when does a piecewise map factor onto an odometer / admit self-induction) that Downarowicz-school and S-adic people could attack with existing tools.
translations.collatz_application: For our certificate program: adopt 'renormalization certificates' — a parametric configuration family C_k, a verified derivation C_k ->* C_{k+1}, and a distinctness lemma — as a first-class certificate type (Coq-BB5's Sk_closed/Base_ne pattern is the reference implementation). This generalizes the linear 'shift rules' already used for Collatz-like TM analyses to geometric/tower families. Honest scope note: for 3x+1 itself such a certificate would prove divergence of an explicit orbit, i.e., disprove Collatz — and the Bernoulli structure of the 2-adic model plus Terras/Tao equidistribution says structured orbits like S_k should not exist; the method's real Collatz-adjacent use is (a) proving divergence for engineered Collatz-like maps and BB(6)-era machines (Antihydra-class), and (b) explaining WHY certain Collatz-like problems are hard: hard holdouts are those whose orbits are random-like (no factor), and the renormalizable ones are exactly the solvable ones — a selection principle for where certificate effort should go.

https://arxiv.org/html/2509.12337v1 (Skelet 17 = 'obfuscated Gray code', Sporadic Machines, irregularity passages)

translations.technique: The Coq formalization pattern: a 7-level certified simulation tower with relations-as-inductive-predicates (mxdys, BB5_Skelet17.v, ~6,900 lines)
translations.what_it_actually_does: mxdys did not transcribe Xu's proof; the md file says it 'adapts' it, and the architecture is an independent redesign: seven explicitly layered abstraction levels (0: raw TM steps, reproved inline rather than lemmatized; 1: '10'-repeater traversals; 2: list rewriting + toConfig bisimulation; 3: Gray decode observables n,s,l + divpow2r bookkeeping; 4: weakly_embanked/embanked composite moves with exact per-coordinate identities carried as hypotheses inside inductive predicates; 5: embanked_batch + Add2s with ctzS scheduling; 6: Base k epoch closure incl. the exceptional ZIHIO move; 7: nonhalt). Each level's transition is an Inductive Prop bundling both the move and its arithmetic postconditions (e.g., weakly_embanked has 6 numbered states s1'-s6' and a dozen equation hypotheses), so the simulation invariant travels inside the constructor. Notable engineering: an inductive wrapper 'Box(P)' exists solely to shield equations from Coq's subst tactic; section-level 'Hypothesis k:nat' abuses hypotheses as parameters; well-formedness is split into two interacting predicates WF1/WF2 whose transitions are tracked to prove one bad configuration shape unreachable; the md admits '6 layers of nested induction'.
translations.math_translation: The implicit content is a composed forward-simulation theorem: there exist relations R_0,...,R_6 between successive abstraction levels such that each R_i is a forward simulation (every abstract move is realized by a nonempty concrete move sequence), and non-halting at level 7 transports to level 0. Formally: if A_7 has an infinite run from alpha_0 and each (A_i, A_{i+1}, R_i) is a sound simulation with R_i relating alpha-images, then the TM has an infinite run from the blank tape — plus the distinctness side condition (Base_ne) to rule out silent halting inside a macro-move. This is refinement/simulation composition exactly as in verified-compiler practice, applied 'upward' (proving liveness of the concrete via the abstract) rather than the usual 'downward' preservation of safety.

nearest_known_math.theory: Forward simulation composition in verified compilation
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by — CompCert's simulation-diagram composition is the same theorem schema; the twist is direction (transporting non-termination down the tower, which requires the diagrams to be productive/nonempty, handled here by measure arguments inside each level)
nearest_known_math.ref: Leroy, 'Formal verification of a realistic compiler', CACM 2009

nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — TryHalve and the composite-move relations are 'up to' constructions; no explicit coinduction is used (the proof is inductive via the k-indexed family)
nearest_known_math.ref: Pous & Sangiorgi, 'Enhancements of the bisimulation proof method', 2011

nearest_known_math.theory: Verified deciders / proof by reflection (the rest of Coq-BB5)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — Skelet17.v is the one place the Coq-BB5 pipeline could NOT use a reflected decider and needed a hand-built mathematical proof; its existence quantifies the boundary of decider technology circa 2024

translations.fusion_opportunity: Extract the tower as a reusable Coq/Lean library: 'certified abstraction towers for one-tape machines' with level templates (repeater compression, list rewriting, observable factor, k-inductive acceleration, renormalization closure). The bbchallenge community will need this industrialized for BB(6) (machines like Antihydra encode genuinely Collatz-like open problems, and hand-rolling 7-level towers per machine does not scale); formal methods people would recognize it as building a domain-specific refinement calculus.
translations.collatz_application: Our certificate program should copy two decisions verbatim: (1) make every certificate an inductive predicate that carries its arithmetic postconditions as constructor hypotheses (so checking = type checking, and composition is mechanical); (2) keep a strict tower discipline — raw map, accelerated map on a section, observable factor, invariant, renormalization family — because that is the interface at which a future 'factor-detection' search tool can plug candidate abstractions in and have them checked. The Skelet17.v file is an existence proof that a ~7,000-line fully-certified semiconjugacy tower for a wild dynamical system is feasible by one person.

weird_unnoticed: The load-bearing fact of the entire endgame — mentioned in no summary, not the wiki, not the BB5 paper's 'obfuscated Gray code' blurb, and only implicitly in Xu's Prop 4.1 — is a PALINDROME SYMMETRY OF THE RULER SEQUENCE. The machine simultaneously runs two odometers in opposite directions: h_1 counts DOWN from 2^{2k}-1 while h_2 counts UP from 2^{2k}, and the coordinate-update schedule alternates between reading v_2 from each. The whole synchronization (Coq: 'the two lines are synchronized (after 4 or 8 embanked_batch) if h_1,h_2 are initialized properly') rests on the pair of lemmas ctzS_add: ctzS(2^i+m)=ctzS(m) and ctzS_sub: ctzS(2^i-m-2)=ctzS(m) (BB5_Skelet17.v:4218-4274, where ctzS(n)=v_2(n+1)) — i.e., the ruler sequence is a palindrome within each dyadic block, because v_2(2^i - x) = v_2(x) for 0 < x < 2^i. So Skelet 17's non-halting ultimately certifies that an odometer and its mirror image, launched from opposite ends of a dyadic block, tick in unison — the machine is a physical implementation of the time-reversal symmetry of the dyadic adding machine. Runner-up oddities: Xu defines Gray code digits by nearest-integer rounding (digit_i = <n/2^i> mod 2, half-up), a closed form I have never seen in print (standard refs use n XOR n>>1); and the Coq file contains an inductive wrapper Box(P) whose only purpose is to hide equations from the subst tactic — arithmetic facts armored against the proof assistant's own automation.
dead_ends: (1) docs.bbchallenge.org is unreachable from this network as warned; Savask's PDF was recovered from the chrisxudoesmath.com mirror instead. (2) The WebFetch summary of the BB5 arXiv HTML misattributed Skelet 17's resolution to the WFAR decider — contradicted by the paper's own Sporadic Machines list and the existence of BB5_Skelet17.v; discounted after cross-checking, but it means the per-decider tables should be re-read directly before quoting. (3) The wiki's claim that Skelet 17 was 'proven to be irregular in August 2024' appears overstated: the BB5 paper only claims 'informal irregularity arguments' for Finned #3 and Skelet #17 — I could not locate a rigorous irregularity proof, so the 'no regular invariant exists' statement should be treated as a well-supported conjecture, not a theorem (and proving it via the Toeplitz-trace route is an open fusion opportunity). (4) Calibration searches for prior art connecting busy beaver holdouts to odometers/Toeplitz systems ('odometer factor Turing machine', 'busy beaver Gray code odometer') returned nothing relevant — the vocabulary gap between the bbchallenge community and the symbolic-dynamics community is real, which is why several verdicts are 'known-math-with-new-twist' rather than 'known-math-reinvented': the pieces are classical but the bridge is unbuilt. (5) Did not page through the local 125-page BB5 PDF after the HTML fetch answered the targeted questions; did not examine busycoq (Skelet 17's formalization lives in Coq-BB5, not busycoq — busycoq's Skelet 1 'cascade' proof would be the natural next domain for this same reverse-translation exercise).
